onboarding.welcomeSubtitle: Let's finish a quick setup so your workspace matches how you work.
onboarding.automation.title: Choose automation level
onboarding.automation.subtitle: How much should AI manage in your projects?
onboarding.automation.full.title: Full Automation
onboarding.automation.full.desc: AI manages tasks, estimates, procurement, and docs. You approve.
onboarding.automation.assisted.title: AI Assisted
onboarding.automation.assisted.desc: AI suggests changes, you review and apply manually.
onboarding.automation.manual.title: Manual
onboarding.automation.manual.desc: You control everything. AI is available on request.
onboarding.automation.observer.title: Observer Only
onboarding.automation.observer.desc: AI provides insights and analytics. No changes proposed.
onboarding.preferences.title: Preferences
onboarding.preferences.subtitle: Set your language and measurement units.
onboarding.project.title: Create your first project
onboarding.project.subtitle: Add a name and type — you can change this later
onboarding.estimate.title: Project stages
onboarding.estimate.subtitle: Add a few meaningful global stages or milestones — for example “Foundation” or “Electrical”. This makes the estimate and tasks easier to manage.
onboarding.estimate.hint: You can add, remove, or rename stages anytime in the Estimate tab.
onboarding.done.title: You're all set!
onboarding.done.subtitle: You can create a project anytime from the home screen
tutorial.estimateFlow.step1.title: The estimate is the project's foundation
tutorial.estimateFlow.step1.description: Add works, materials, and rates. Everything flows into Tasks and Procurement.
tutorial.estimateFlow.step2.title: 'In work' status is the key moment
tutorial.estimateFlow.step2.description: Tasks and Procurement stay empty while the estimate is in Planning. Switch to In work — and everything lights up.
tutorial.estimateFlow.step3.title: Client price
tutorial.estimateFlow.step3.description: The estimate keeps two prices: your internal cost and the client price. Clients only see their price.
tutorial.aiSidebar.step1.title: AI Assistant
tutorial.aiSidebar.step1.description: Ask questions about the project — the assistant knows your estimate, tasks, procurement, and team. Answers are context-aware.
tutorial.aiSidebar.step1.bubble: How much tile do we need?
tutorial.aiSidebar.step2.title: Save useful answers
tutorial.aiSidebar.step2.description: Click the bookmark under an answer — it lands in the project knowledge base and shows up in Documents.
tutorial.aiSidebar.step2.answerPreview: For a 10 m² kitchen, buy 12 m² of tile to cover cuts.
tutorial.aiSidebar.step3.title: Photo consultation
tutorial.aiSidebar.step3.description: Attach a photo from the site — the assistant analyzes it and suggests what to add to the estimate or tasks.
tutorial.documents.step1.title: Project documents
tutorial.documents.step1.description: Store, print, and download contracts, estimates, acts, and drawings — all in one place.
tutorial.documents.step2.title: Internal and shared documents
tutorial.documents.step2.description: Mark documents as shared to show a floor plan to the client, or internal — like a window-installation guide for contractors.
tutorial.media.step1.title: Site photos
tutorial.media.step1.description: Upload photos per task. Contractors attach a photo when finishing work; the AI assistant reads images and suggests estimate changes.
tutorial.media.step1.aiHint: AI can read photos and suggest what's still pending

estimate.tooltip.durationPlanned: Planned calendar days between scheduled start and end dates.
estimate.tooltip.durationEstimated: Estimated calendar days based on current progress and velocity, if available.
estimate.tooltip.daysToEnd: Days remaining until scheduled end date.
estimate.tooltip.behindSchedule: How many days the estimated finish exceeds the planned finish.
estimate.footer.helperTooltip: Review total cost, client total and VAT for the full estimate.
estimate.delete.desc.simple.resource: This resource will be removed from the estimate. This action cannot be undone.
estimate.delete.desc.simple.work: This work and all resources inside it will be removed from the estimate. This action cannot be undone.
estimate.delete.desc.simple.stage: This stage and all works and resources inside it will be removed from the estimate. This action cannot be undone.
estimate.delete.desc.execution.work.done: This work is already completed. Deleting it will discard estimate context for work that has already been executed.
estimate.delete.desc.execution.work.inPr: This work is already in progress. Deleting it will discard estimate context for work that has already started.
estimate.delete.desc.execution.work.bloc: This work has already started and is currently blocked. Deleting it will discard estimate context for that execution.
estimate.delete.desc.execution.work.star: This work has already started. Deleting it will discard estimate context for that execution.
estimate.delete.desc.execution.stage: This stage already contains work that has started and is not Done. Review the list below before you continue.
estimate.delete.desc.financial: Deleting this {{entity}} will remove estimate context that is already tied to ordered, stocked, or paid work and materials. This can affect financial and operational records, and the data cannot be recovered through the normal UI flow.
estimate.delete.startedHeading: Started work and tasks in this stage
estimate.delete.hrHeading: HR and payment records already affected

landing.brand.name: StroyAgent
landing.betaBar.text: Rovno is in open beta. Full functionality is free, with no project limits.
landing.hero.title: Construction where everything is in its place
landing.hero.subtitle: Rovno brings tasks, estimates, procurement, photos, and documents into a single workspace. AI turns your brief into a structured project in minutes, so you manage construction, not chaos.
landing.demos.title: See a sample workspace
landing.demos.subtitle: Ready-made apartment, office, and landscape projects. Walk through the interface without signing up.
landing.howItWorks.title: How it works
landing.howItWorks.subtitle: From initial idea to execution control in three predictable steps.
landing.howItWorks.steps.describe.title: Describe the work
landing.howItWorks.steps.describe.line1: AI converts text into stages, tasks, and checklists.
landing.howItWorks.steps.describe.line2: You start with structure, not a blank board.
landing.howItWorks.steps.attach.title: Attach evidence
landing.howItWorks.steps.attach.line1: Plans, photos, estimates, invoices. Everything organized.
landing.howItWorks.steps.attach.line2: Plans, photos, estimates, and invoices are neatly filed into the right project sections.
landing.howItWorks.steps.control.title: Control execution
landing.howItWorks.steps.control.line1: Statuses, blockers, procurement, and progress by stage.
landing.howItWorks.steps.control.line2: Every update stays visible to the team.
landing.control.title: Everything in one workspace
landing.control.subtitle: Tasks, estimates, procurement, photos, and documents stay in sync.

landing.estimate.heading: Estimate
landing.estimate.items.drywall.note: Additional corner beads and extra sanding after wall irregularities.
landing.estimate.items.drywall.aiInsight: AI: Variance is consistent with extra prep on uneven substrates. Next time: add a 10% contingency on finishing for old-panel walls.
landing.estimate.items.drywall.statusIns: Additional finishing prep increased labor and material scope.
landing.estimate.items.tiles.statusInsig: Client layout confirmation pending before grouting.
landing.photos.toast.photoRequested: Request sent: upload close-up seam photos.
landing.photos.toast.projectDraft: Project draft prepared in the prompt above.
landing.photos.landscapePrompt: Create a landscape design project for my vacation house
landing.photos.items.tile.title: Tile lippage check
landing.photos.items.tile.aiQuestion: Check this tile installation. Is the lippage acceptable and what's the correct fix?
landing.photos.items.tile.aiReason: Lippage and grout alignment appear outside acceptable finishing tolerance in the visible zone.
landing.photos.items.tile.finding1: Edge offset appears above target plane on multiple adjacent tiles.
landing.photos.items.tile.finding2: Grout line width variation indicates inconsistent bedding depth.
landing.photos.items.tile.finding3: Defect location increases slip and moisture-retention risk.
landing.photos.items.tile.evidence1: 2 m straightedge photo with feeler gauge reading.
landing.photos.items.tile.evidence2: Close-up of three worst joints before removal.
landing.photos.items.tile.evidence3: Adhesive coverage photo after first tile lift.
landing.photos.items.tile.createTask1: Create task: Re-bed raised corridor tiles.
landing.photos.items.tile.createTask3: Set acceptance gate: max 1 mm lippage before signoff.
landing.photos.items.tile.order1: Order leveling clips and wedges for one correction cycle.
landing.photos.items.tile.order2: Add fresh C2-class adhesive and replacement spacers.
landing.photos.items.tile.order3: Confirm delivery before rework slot starts.
landing.photos.items.tile.reminder1: Request contractor quote for affected square meterage.
landing.photos.items.tile.reminder2: Include removal, reset, and cleanup in one line item.
landing.photos.items.tile.reminder3: Set reminder to review quote same day.
landing.photos.items.crack.title: Window corner crack
landing.photos.items.crack.aiQuestion: Hairline crack near window corner. Cause and best repair approach?
landing.photos.items.crack.aiReason: Pattern matches movement stress near opening corner with likely weak local reinforcement.
landing.photos.items.crack.finding1: Crack trajectory follows a high-stress corner path from the window edge.
landing.photos.items.crack.finding2: No visible branching suggests localized finish-layer issue.
landing.photos.items.crack.finding3: Repair should include mesh reinforcement to prevent recurrence.
landing.photos.items.crack.evidence1: Corner photo after V-groove opening and cleaning.
landing.photos.items.crack.evidence2: Photo confirming mesh overlap beyond crack line.
landing.photos.items.crack.createTask1: Create task: Repair window corner crack.
landing.photos.items.crack.createTask2: Sequence: groove, mesh, flexible filler, repaint.
landing.photos.items.crack.createTask3: Set check: no re-opening after first week.
landing.photos.items.crack.order1: Order fiberglass mesh tape and elastic filler.
landing.photos.items.crack.order2: Include fine-grit sanding sheets and primer.
landing.photos.items.crack.order3: Reserve enough material for two passes.
landing.photos.items.crack.reminder2: Check if crack width changes after repaint.
landing.photos.items.crack.reminder3: Escalate to frame anchoring check if reopened.
landing.photos.items.insulation.title: Insulation assembly ID
landing.photos.items.insulation.aiQuesti: What materials are shown and is the vapor barrier installed correctly?
landing.photos.items.insulation.aiReason: Assembly appears correct in principle, but seam continuity is not fully verifiable from this angle.
landing.photos.items.insulation.finding1: Mineral wool infill between studs appears consistent (~100 mm class).
landing.photos.items.insulation.finding2: Polyethylene barrier is present but seam taping is incomplete.
landing.photos.items.insulation.finding3: Untaped joints may reduce thermal performance and raise condensation risk.
landing.photos.items.insulation.evidence: Close-up photos of all membrane seams.
Corner and outlet penetration sealing photos.
One full-bay continuity photo before cladding.
landing.photos.items.insulation.createTa: Create QA checklist task for barrier continuity.
Add checkpoints for seams, corners, and penetrations.
Require signoff photos before wall closure.
landing.photos.items.insulation.order1: Order additional sealing tape for all exposed joints.
landing.photos.items.insulation.order2: Include membrane patch material for puncture repair.
landing.photos.items.insulation.photo1: Request close-up seam photos every 2-3 meters.
landing.photos.items.insulation.photo2: Request outlet and corner photos with tape visible.
landing.photos.items.insulation.photo3: Mark each photo by room and wall segment.
landing.photos.items.landscape.title: Vacation house landscape plan
landing.photos.items.landscape.aiQuestio: Plan a practical landscape design around this vacation house. Prioritize drainage, low maintenance, and a clean modern look.
landing.photos.items.landscape.aiReason: Site is suitable for phased low-maintenance landscape delivery with drainage-first sequencing.
landing.photos.items.landscape.finding1: Drainage correction should precede planting and paving to avoid rework.
landing.photos.items.landscape.finding2: Simple modern hardscape geometry suits current house lines.
landing.photos.items.landscape.finding3: Native layered planting can reduce upkeep while keeping year-round structure.
landing.photos.items.landscape.evidence1: Quick slope map with low-point markers.
landing.photos.items.landscape.evidence2: Foundation distance check for planned planting zones.
landing.photos.items.landscape.evidence3: Night photo to place low-voltage lighting routes.
landing.photos.items.landscape.project1: Create landscape project with 5 phases: survey, drainage, hardscape, planting, lighting.
landing.photos.items.landscape.project2: Set phase gates and acceptance criteria per stage.
landing.photos.items.landscape.project3: Prefill kickoff prompt for immediate planning.
landing.photos.items.landscape.materials: Generate materials list by phase and supplier category.
Separate drainage, hardscape, and planting packages.
Include contingency line for soil amendments.
landing.photos.items.landscape.plan2: Reserve first week for survey and drainage setup.
landing.photos.items.landscape.plan3: Set handoff checkpoints to avoid scope drift.
landing.doc.runScanHint: Run scan to detect risks and generate corrective edits.
landing.doc.findings.scope.title: Scope is open-ended
landing.doc.findings.scope.description: Undefined deliverables can create billing disputes and quality disagreements.
landing.doc.findings.milestones.title: Milestones are not fixed
landing.doc.findings.milestones.descript: Missing dates and acceptance gates weaken schedule accountability.
landing.doc.findings.penalty.title: Penalty is one-sided
landing.doc.findings.penalty.description: Only the client is exposed to delay penalties.
landing.doc.findings.payment.title: Payment terms are unbalanced
landing.doc.findings.payment.description: Large upfront payment and broad suspension rights increase client risk.
landing.doc.findings.variation.title: Variation control is missing
landing.doc.findings.variation.descripti: Extra work can be billed without prior commercial approval.
landing.doc.findings.warranty.title: Warranty period is ambiguous
landing.doc.findings.warranty.descriptio: Undefined duration and remedy process create ambiguity after handover.
landing.doc.findings.termination.title: Termination right is unilateral
landing.doc.findings.termination.descrip: Contractor can exit quickly without mirrored protection for the client.
landing.doc.findings.dispute.title: Dispute venue is unfair
landing.doc.findings.dispute.description: Single-party venue control raises enforcement burden.
landing.doc.clauses.scope.original: The Contractor shall perform renovation works to modernize the apartment interiors as reasonably required to deliver a complete result acceptable to the Client.
landing.doc.clauses.scope.suggested: The Contractor shall perform only the works listed in Appendix A (Scope Matrix), including stated quantities, materials, and exclusions; any additional deliverable requires a signed variation order before execution.
landing.doc.clauses.milestones.original: The Works shall be completed in phases according to a mutually agreed schedule, with milestone dates to be coordinated during project execution.
landing.doc.clauses.milestones.suggested: The Works shall follow the milestone schedule in Appendix B with fixed target dates, acceptance criteria for each stage, and a written change log for any approved timeline adjustments.
landing.doc.clauses.payment.original: The Client shall pay a 70% advance within three business days of signing, with the remaining amount payable upon final completion; delayed payments permit immediate suspension of all works.
landing.doc.clauses.payment.suggested: Payments shall be stage-based: 20% mobilization, 30% after rough-in acceptance, 30% after finishing acceptance, and 20% at final handover; suspension may occur only after a 7-day cure notice for undisputed amounts.
landing.doc.clauses.penalty.original: Any delay by the Client in approvals, access, or payments shall incur a penalty of 0.5% of the contract value per day, and no equivalent penalty shall apply to the Contractor.
landing.doc.clauses.penalty.suggested: Delay penalties shall be reciprocal: each party pays 0.2% of affected stage value per day for delays within its control, capped at 10% of the relevant stage value.
landing.doc.clauses.variation.original: Additional works requested by the Client may be executed upon instruction and charged at the Contractor's standard rates without requiring a signed variation order.
landing.doc.clauses.variation.suggested: No variation shall be executed unless both parties sign a written variation order specifying scope, price, and schedule impact, except emergency safety works documented within 24 hours.
landing.doc.clauses.warranty.original: The Contractor provides a workmanship warranty for a reasonable period after completion, subject to normal use and maintenance conditions.
landing.doc.clauses.warranty.suggested: The Contractor provides a 12-month workmanship warranty from handover; defects reported in writing shall be inspected within 5 business days and remedied within a mutually agreed corrective timeline.
landing.doc.clauses.termination.original: The Contractor may terminate this Agreement at any time with two days' written notice, with payment due for all completed and planned works up to the termination date.
landing.doc.clauses.termination.suggeste: Either party may terminate for material breach after a 10-day cure period; payment on termination is limited to accepted completed works and documented demobilization costs.
landing.doc.clauses.dispute.original: All disputes shall be resolved exclusively by the courts located at the Contractor's registered office, and the Client waives objection to venue.
landing.doc.clauses.dispute.suggested: Disputes shall first be escalated to executive negotiation for 15 days, then submitted to arbitration in a mutually agreed neutral venue under agreed procedural rules.

pricing.title: Pricing
pricing.subtitle: Open beta is in progress. Paid tiers will come later — for now, it's free.
pricing.tooltips.freeKb: A consolidated library of building codes, regulations, legal acts, engineering standards, manufacturer specifications, and proven industry best practices for construction, renovation, and landscaping.
pricing.tooltips.credits: Credits are used for AI actions across the product. Choose a monthly bundle based on your workload. Daily credits are added on top.
pricing.plans.free.name: Free
pricing.plans.free.description: Full functionality during beta with partial AI access.
pricing.plans.free.betaNote: During beta, full functionality is open for free: no project limits and partial beta access to the AI assistant.
